Index.title: Find a home for your food scraps, wherever you are
Index.subtitle: Peels connects folks with food scraps to those who compost. It’s a free, non-commercial, community project.
Index.metaDescription: Peels connects folks with food scraps to those who compost. It’s a free, non-commercial, community project.
Index.metaKeywords: food waste, food scraps, share waste, sharewaste, makesoil, compost near me, compost drop-off, food scrap drop-off
Index.hostAvatarAlt: The avatar for a Peels host

howItWorks.title: Here’s how it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Sharing food scraps with neighbours, community gardens, or even local businesses is easy. Here’s how.
howItWorks.footer: That’s all there is to it! <page>Get out there</page> and meet your neighbours.

findAHost.title: Find a host
findAHost.subtitle: Select a marker on the map to see who’s nearby.

contact.title: Contact
contact.subtitle: Arrange to drop-off or collect your scraps via chat.

dropOff.title: Drop-off
dropOff.subtitle: Or collect, if you’ve reached out to a local business with scraps to give away.

newsletter.title: What’s new
newsletter.subtitle: Occasional updates on how Peels is supporting community composting around the world.
newsletter.footer: Check out our <page>Newsletter</page> page for all past issues and how to subscribe.

partners.title: In good company
partners.subtitle: Peels is recommended by local governments, community partners, and the press.
partners.footer: And many more. See our <page>Partners</page> page for the full list.

faq.title: You might be wondering
faq.subtitle: How does Peels work? Who’s behind it, and who is it for? Here are some answers.
faq.footer: Still have questions? <page>Read on</page> for more.

Partners.title: Partners
Partners.subtitle: Governments, community partners, and publications working with Peels so more food scraps stay out of landfill.
Partners.metaDescription: Meet the governments, community partners, and publications pointing people to Peels for practical composting options.

communityPartners.title: Community partners
communityPartners.subtitle: Organisations helping more people discover practical, community-led composting through Peels.

loccal.description: The Local Community Compost Alliance of Australia supports community-led and community-operated composting across Australia.

responsiblecafes.description: Responsible Cafes connects eco-conscious Australians with sustainable cafes working to eliminate single-use plastic coffee cups.

councilMentions.title: Government mentions

cta.title: Want to see Peels in your community?
cta.body: Start with our <share>Share</share> page, or <email>email us</email> if you need something more specific for your council, community group, or organisation.

Share.title: Share
Share.heading: Spread the word
Share.subtitle: Resources for councils, community groups, cafes, and neighbours helping more people find community composting.
Share.metaDescription: Download Peels sharing resources for councils, community groups, cafes, and neighbours helping more people find community composting.
Share.downloadHint: One ZIP with social tiles, print assets, and workshop-friendly resources.

resources.title: Inside the ZIP
resources.subtitle: A simple bundle of ready-to-use visuals and print materials for your local community.

digital.title: Digital tiles
digital.body: Square and portrait graphics for Instagram, newsletters, and community noticeboards.

print.title: Print assets
print.body: Posters, letterbox drops, signs, and stickers for cafes, libraries, markets, and shared spaces.

workshop.title: Workshop resources
workshop.body: Display-friendly pieces for stalls, workshops, and community events where people can learn about Peels in person.

copyExamples.title: Copy examples
copyExamples.subtitle: Please feel free to adapt any of these options to fit your needs. They’re provided simply as starting points, at various lengths.

shortest.title: Shortest
shortest.body: Peels connects folks with food scraps to those who compost.

medium.title: Medium
medium.body: Peels connects folks with food scraps to those who can compost them. Go to peels.org to find (or become) a neighbour or community garden that accepts food scraps near you.

long.title: Long
long.body: Looking for a compost drop-off? Find a neighbour or community garden that accepts food scraps via Peels. Can’t produce enough compost of your own, or want to see less food waste go to landfill? Create a listing to accept scraps from neighbours. Learn more at peels.org.

copyExamples.partnersNote: See our <partners>Partners page</partners> for real-world examples of how councils and organisations talk about Peels.

Support.title: Contact
Support.metaDescription: Answers and contact options for Peels.
Support.subtitle: Feel free to email us about anything, or browse the FAQs below for answers to the basics.

whatIsPeels.question: What is Peels?
whatIsPeels.answer: Peels is a community composting map that helps people find nearby places to drop off food scraps, or create a listing if they can accept scraps from neighbours. It’s a free, non-commercial, community project to connect folks with food scraps to those who compost.

whosBehind.question: Who maintains Peels?
whosBehind.answer:
Peels is maintained by volunteers and contributors who want to help more food scraps become compost instead of landfill.

The project was started by Danny White, who helped begin the composting program at Pocket City Farms. Peels is open source, and contributions are welcome.

howDifferent.question: How is Peels different to ShareWaste?
howDifferent.answer:
ShareWaste shut down at the end of 2024 but helped popularise a similar idea: connect people with food scraps to nearby people who compost. Peels carries that idea forward as an actively maintained, open-source platform.

You can still find compost hosts on a map, create a listing, and arrange food-scrap drop-offs or pickups with nearby members.

findDropOff.question: How do I find a compost drop-off near me?
findDropOff.answer: Open the Peels map and look for a nearby host or community garden that accepts food scraps. Select a listing to see what they accept, where they are, and how to contact them.

noGarden.question: Can I compost food scraps if I don’t have a garden or compost bin?
noGarden.answer: Yes. Peels is designed for people who have food scraps but nowhere practical to compost them. You can use Peels to find someone nearby who already composts and is open to receiving scraps.

businesses.question: Can businesses use Peels to donate food scraps?
businesses.answer: Yes. Cafes, restaurants, offices, grocers, and other businesses can use Peels if they have food scraps that nearby composters could collect. Create a business listing so people can see what you have, where you are, and how to arrange pickup.

financialModel.question: What’s the financial model? Are you non-profit?
financialModel.answer: Peels is a non-commercial, community-led project. We may incorporate as a not-for-profit in the future and accept grant funding or sponsorships for further development, but we never intend to start charging for the service.

fogo.question: I already have food scrap collection. Is community composting relevant?
fogo.answer: Yes. Community composting means food scraps are composted by neighbours, community gardens, local businesses, or other local projects, not industrial processes or landfill. Many people still don’t have access to reliable food-scrap collection, and community composting can also be more local, social, and educational than a council or city-wide service. Peels is designed to support both the places that already have collection and the places still building it.

mapPrivacy.question: I’m not comfortable sharing my address. Can I still participate?
mapPrivacy.answer:
Yes! We encourage folks with residential listings to ‘roughen’ their location to a nearby street corner or similar, and use a pseudonym if they feel more comfortable doing so.

Even if you choose to use your real name and upload a photo, only signed in Peels members can see those details.

getInvolved.question: I’d like to help build Peels. How do I get involved?
getInvolved.answer:
You’re awesome. Thank you.

If you’re inclined to help on the technical side, check out our GitHub repo. It has information on how to contribute, plus some existing issues that could do with your eyes.

Community organising is also a big part of getting Peels off the ground. Please don’t hesistate to email us if that’s something you could help with.

promotion.question: How can I promote Peels to my community?
promotion.answer:
First off, thank you! Peels only works with a thriving map of listings, so getting the word out is crucial.

Head to our Share page for social tiles, printable posters, and copy examples you can adapt for your community. Don’t hesitate to reach out if you need something more specific.

government.question: I represent a government or institution. How can we get involved?
government.answer:
We’re keen to work with public-sector teams, community organisations, and educators anywhere Peels can help more people keep food scraps out of landfill. Our Partners page shows how other councils mention Peels, and our Share page has copy and assets you can adapt.

Please email us if you need something more specific for your community.

manageEmails.question: How do I manage which emails I receive?
manageEmails.answer:
Peels sends account and message emails that are necessary to keep the service working, including sign-in emails and notifications when another member messages you.

The newsletter is separate and optional. You can subscribe or unsubscribe at any time from your Profile page.

If you host a listing, message notifications are required so prospective donors can reach you. You can stop new enquiries by hiding your listing from the map or deleting it.

You can also report or block individual Peels members via the messaging system.
